summary: For an amalgam restoration requiring precise fitment into larger buccolingual areas such as seen in lower molars post-cavity preparation involving extensive removal or damage affecting both mesial/distal aspects, employing double rather than singular wedges enhances control over placement while improving seal integrity crucially important during occlusal function cycles. This method ensures better adaptation around adjacent tooth surfaces minimizing potential complications associated with improper marginal sealing typical pitfalls encountered using simpler techniques alone. Agent2 concludes that given these factors favoring enhanced accuracy and effectiveness through multi-step application compared to traditional methods; therefore recommending adoption of dual versus solitary wedge strategies specifically tailored toward expansive preparations necessitating meticulous attention throughout treatment planning stages leading up actual execution phases within oral healthcare settings focusing heavily upon achieving optimal outcomes via minimally invasive yet highly effective means possible today leveraging modern materials alongside advanced procedural
